explanation: ЩҒШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Щ„Ш°ШӘ ЩҲ ШӘЩҒШұЫҢШӯ Ш§ЩҒШұШ§ШҜ Ш§ЩҶШ¬Ш§Щ… Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜШӣ ШҜШұ Щ…ШӘЩҶ ШЁЩҮ ЩҮШҜЩҒ Ш§ЩҲЩ„ЫҢЩҮ ШЁШ§ШәвҖҢЩҲШӯШҙвҖҢЩҮШ§ Ш§ШҙШ§ШұЩҮ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ.

.
The first zoo to focus on protecting animals was started by 2

.
Since the 1990s a number of zoos have introduced programmes which aim to

explanation: ШЁЩҮ Щ…Ш№ЩҶШ§ЫҢ ЩҶЪҜЩҮ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘЩҶ ШӯЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶШ§ШӘ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШӘЩҲЩ„ЫҢШҜ Щ…Ш«Щ„ШҢ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩҲЫҢЪҳЩҮ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШӯЩҒШё ЪҜЩҲЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ Ш§ЩҶЩӮШұШ§Ш¶.
ЪҜЩҲЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ Ш¬Щ…Ш№ЫҢШӘ ШўЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁЩҮ ШҙШҜШӘ Ъ©Ш§ЩҮШҙ ЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘЩҮ ЩҲ ШҜШұ Ш®Ш·Шұ Ш§ЩҶЩӮШұШ§Ш¶ ЩҮШіШӘЩҶШҜ.

text: animals and return them to their 3

explanation: Щ…ШӯЫҢШ·ЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ ЫҢЪ© ШӯЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶ ЫҢШ§ ЪҜЫҢШ§ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ Ш·ЩҲШұ Ш·ШЁЫҢШ№ЫҢ ШҜШұ ШўЩҶ ШІЩҶШҜЪҜЫҢ ЩҲ ШұШҙШҜ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ.

text: . But first the animals have to learn how to survive in the 4

explanation: Щ…ШӯЫҢШ·ЫҢ Ш·ШЁЫҢШ№ЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ ШӘШӯШӘ Ъ©ЩҶШӘШұЩ„ ЫҢШ§ Щ…ШұШ§ЩӮШЁШӘ Ш§ЩҶШіШ§ЩҶ ЩҶЫҢШіШӘ.
ШҜШұ Щ…ШӯЫҢШ· ШЁШ§ШәвҖҢЩҲШӯШҙШҢ ШЁЩҮ ЩҒШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ ЫҢШ§ Ш§ШЁШІШ§ШұЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ ЪҜЩҒШӘЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШӘШӯШұЫҢЪ© Ш°ЩҮЩҶЫҢ ЩҲ ЩҒЫҢШІЫҢЪ©ЫҢ ШӯЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶШ§ШӘ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ.

text: by making it challenging for them to find their food, providing them with climbing equipment, and even mixing them with other

explanation: ШҜШіШӘЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ Ш§ШІ Ш¬Ш§ЩҶШҜШ§ШұШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЩҮ ЩҲЫҢЪҳЪҜЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШҙШ§ШЁЩҮЫҢ ШҜШ§ШұЩҶШҜ ЩҲ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЩҶШҜ ШЁШ§ ЩҮЩ… ШӘЩҲЩ„ЫҢШҜ Щ…Ш«Щ„ Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜ.
ШҜЩҲШұШЁЫҢЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШӘШөЩ„ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ЫҢЩҶШӘШұЩҶШӘ Ъ©ЩҮ Ш§Ш¬Ш§ШІЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜ ШӘШөШ§ЩҲЫҢШұ ШІЩҶШҜЩҮ Ш§ШІ Щ…ШӯЫҢШ·ЫҢ (Щ…Ш«Щ„ ЩӮЩҒШі ШӯЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶШ§ШӘ) ЩҫШ®Шҙ ШҙЩҲШҜ.

text: enable the public to view zoo animals from their homes.

Complete each sentence with the correct ending, A-H.
7 The first zoo
8 The World Association for Zoos and Aquariums (WAZA)
9 Borneo Orangutan Rescue
10 Colchester Zoo
11 Indianapolis Zoo
A has created an environment that copies the animals' natural habitat.
B was started by Gerald Durrell.
C has webcams in all the enclosures.
D prepares one species to return to their own environment.
E does not always separate different types of animal.
F is part of the AZA.
G is involved in teaching about how to better care for animals and protect them and in helping zoos work together.
H was a private collection of animals.

The first zoo is believed to have been in Egypt in around 3500 BC. Early collections of animals on display often belonged to royalty. King Henry I of England had lions, leopards and camels. He had received them as gifts. Until the late twentieth century, the main purpose of zoos was for entertainment. They existed to give people the chance to see animals they could not see in the wild. In 1959, famous wildlife expert Gerald Durrell opened the first zoo which put

explanation: Щ…ШӯШ§ЩҒШёШӘ Ш§ШІ Щ…ШӯЫҢШ· ШІЫҢШіШӘ ЩҲ ШӯЫҢШ§ШӘ ЩҲШӯШҙ ШҜШұ ШЁШұШ§ШЁШұ ШўШіЫҢШЁ ЫҢШ§ Ш§ЩҶЩӮШұШ§Ш¶.

text:
of animals first.

Nowadays zoos' aims are: conservation, education, entertainment, in that order. Modern zoos try to get a balance between providing safe, comfortable homes for the animals and opportunities for visitors to see animals in natural surroundings. Zoos now share their knowledge and support each other. The World Association for Zoos and Aquariums (WAZA) works to educate people about animal

explanation: ШіЩ„Ш§Щ…ШӘШҢ ШұШ§ШӯШӘЫҢ ЩҲ ШҙШ§ШҜЫҢ ШӯЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶШ§ШӘШӣ ШҜШұ Ш§ЫҢЩҶШ¬Ш§ ШЁЩҮ ШҙШұШ§ЫҢШ· ШІЩҶШҜЪҜЫҢ ШўЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШҜШұ Ш§ШіШ§ШұШӘ Ш§ШҙШ§ШұЩҮ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ.

text: and conservation. It also helps to

explanation: ШіШ§ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶШҜЩҮЫҢ Ш§ЩҒШұШ§ШҜ ЫҢШ§ ЪҜШұЩҲЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…Ш®ШӘЩ„ЩҒ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩ…Ъ©Ш§ШұЫҢ Щ…ЩҲШ«Шұ ШЁШ§ ЫҢЪ©ШҜЫҢЪҜШұ.

text:
breeding programmes.

From the 1990s, zoos began to see their main role as saving the most threatened species. Each zoo focuses on a small number of species. The Siberian tiger and the orangutan are two species that have been saved through breeding programmes run by zoos. For example, in 2005 there were between 331 and 393 Siberian tigers in the Russian Far East. By 2015, there were 562. The tigers were helped to breed under the Species Survival Plan of the Association of Zoos and Aquariums (AZA). Often the aim is to

explanation: ЩҒШұШўЫҢЩҶШҜ ШЁШ§ШІЪҜШұШҜШ§ЩҶШҜЩҶ ШӯЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶШ§ШӘ Ш§ШІ Ш§ШіШ§ШұШӘ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ШӯЫҢШ· Ш·ШЁЫҢШ№ЫҢ Ш§ШөЩ„ЫҢвҖҢШҙШ§ЩҶ.
ЪҜЩҲЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁШ§ Ш®Ш·Шұ ШЁШіЫҢШ§Шұ ШЁШ§Щ„Ш§ЫҢ ЩҶШ§ШЁЩҲШҜЫҢ ШҜШұ ШўЫҢЩҶШҜЩҮ ЩҶШІШҜЫҢЪ© ШұЩҲШЁШұЩҲ ЩҮШіШӘЩҶШҜ.

text:
species into their natural habitats. In Indonesia, Borneo Orangutan Rescue teaches orangutans how to live in the wild. When they are ready, they are taken to protected forests to live naturally.

Now that we have a better understanding of animals, zoos try to recreate natural habitats of animals as accurately as possible. For example, polar bears live in a plain, white open space. Zoos try to provide 'enrichment' for the animals to improve their wellbeing. This includes climbing frames, feeding puzzles, unusual objects and different types of food. It allows animals to behave in a normal way, such as scratching, climbing, digging and solving problems. Another type of enrichment is mixing up types of animal that would naturally mix in the wild. Colchester Zoo in England has a 'kingdom of the wild'

explanation: ЫҢЪ© ЩҒШ¶Ш§ЫҢ ШЁШ§ШІ ЩҲ Щ…ШӯШөЩҲШұ ШҙШҜЩҮ ШҜШұ ШЁШ§ШәвҖҢЩҲШӯШҙ Ъ©ЩҮ ШӯЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶШ§ШӘ ШҜШұ ШўЩҶ ЩҶЪҜЩҮШҜШ§ШұЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲЩҶШҜ.

text:
where several species live together. They have found positive changes to the behaviour of these animals as a result.

Different types of technology are used to help create natural environments for animals. In Indianapolis Zoo, in the United States, for example, there is a 'functional forest' called the Hutan Trail, a series of towers and cables 20 metres above the ground. This allows the orangutans to swing around the zoo as they would swing around the forest in their native Indonesia. The cables are strong enough to give them a safe and comfortable trip around the zoo. They have access to three oases, where they can go when they want to be apart from the group.

Likewise, technology has been used to

explanation: Ш¬Щ„ШЁ ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮ ЫҢШ§ Ш№Щ„Ш§ЩӮЩҮ Ъ©ШіЫҢШӣ ШҜШұ Ш§ЫҢЩҶШ¬Ш§ ШЁЩҮ Щ…Ш№ЩҶШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШҙШ§ШұЪ©ШӘ ШҜШ§ШҜЩҶ Щ…ШұШҜЩ… ШҜШұ Щ…ЩҲШ¶ЩҲШ№Ш§ШӘ Щ…ШұШЁЩҲШ· ШЁЩҮ ШӯЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶШ§ШӘ Ш§ШіШӘ.

text: the public more with the animals. In many zoos there are webcams in some

explanation: Щ…ЩҶШ§Ш·ЩӮЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ ШӘЩҲШіШ· Щ…Ш§ЩҶШ№ ЫҢШ§ ШҜЫҢЩҲШ§Шұ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ЩҶЪҜЩҮШҜШ§ШұЫҢ ШӯЫҢЩҲШ§ЩҶШ§ШӘ Щ…ШӯШөЩҲШұ ШҙШҜЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜ.

text:
so visitors can keep up with their favourite animals between visits to the zoo.

Zoos have moved on over the last few decades. They now play an important role in protecting animals and in making people more aware of the importance of animals. Zoos have made people aware that animals should not be over-hunted and that they play an important role in the

explanation: ШұШ§ШЁШ·ЩҮ ШЁЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜШ§ШӘ ШІЩҶШҜЩҮ ЩҲ Щ…ШӯЫҢШ· Ш§Ш·ШұШ§ЩҒ ШўЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§.

text: of the planet and in helping to maintain the balance of nature.
